title: Scene
description: A complete snapshot of "what to render."

signals.description: Last-known value for each referenced signal topic. Compositors that support push-based updates MAY subscribe and ignore this cache; compositors that only pull SHOULD treat this as the authoritative initial value.

ActionKind.description: What the action *means*. Compositors choose representation; agents react by semantics.

oneOf.description: Submit a payload back to the agent — the most generic action.
Request the agent inspect another node (drill-down, "tell me more").
Move focus to another node without side effects.
Invoke a named command on the agent (e.g. "rerun", "cancel").
Record human feedback on a result — thumb-up/down plus optional comment.
Pick one of the options declared by a `Choice` intent.
Fill an `Input` intent's value.
Confirm a `Confirm` intent.

ActionSlot.description:
An interactive slot attached to a node.

Slots are *declarative* — the node says "this action is available," the compositor decides how to present it (button, keybind, voice command, gesture), and the agent reacts to the emitted `ActionEvent`.

BindTarget.description: What part of a node a binding updates.

oneOf.description: Update a named attribute on the node (e.g. `"pct"`, `"label"`).
Update a named slot inside the node's `Intent` payload (e.g. `"progress.pct"`).
Replace the content of a child node entirely.

Binding.description:
Binds a live signal to a rendering slot on a node.

The compositor is responsible for subscribing to the signal and applying the (optional) transform before updating the target.

ChoiceOption.description: One of the options presented by a `Choice` intent.

id.description: Stable identifier used in the corresponding `ActionKind::Choose`.

Density.description: Visual density preference. Compositors interpret this per-surface.

FileWriteKind.description:
What kind of filesystem write a `FileWrite` intent represents.

`#[non_exhaustive]` so future variants (e.g. `Patch` for diff-based writes) land additively.

oneOf.description: New file at `path`. The write MUST fail if the file already exists.
Overwrite an existing file at `path`. Compositors MAY render as a `mod`/`modified` badge.
Append content to the end of an existing file.
Remove the file at `path`. `content` SHOULD be absent.

FormationKind.description: A coherent grouping of agents — renders as a cluster, swarm, or quorum.

oneOf.description: Threshold-reached consensus.
Moving coordinated group.
Static ring, line, or geometric arrangement.
Leftover scent traces (stigmergic).

GroupKind.description: How a `Group` lays out its children.

oneOf.description: Top-to-bottom list.
Gridded layout — compositors decide column count.
Strictly ordered sequence — render as a process or timeline.
Concurrent/parallel items — render side-by-side or overlapped.
A stack — only the top child is fully visible.

Intent.description:
The semantic type of a node.

See module docs for the design philosophy. Compositors SHOULD handle every variant gracefully; falling back to rendering via `Intent::Prose` of the variant's `Display` is acceptable when no better representation is available.

oneOf.description: Plain semantic prose. Inline markup is NOT interpreted — compositors format according to the surrounding node's attributes.
Source code in a named language. Compositors MAY syntax-highlight.
Mathematical expression in LaTeX or MathML. Optional; text compositors fall back to raw source.
A pointer to a known entity in the agent's knowledge graph. Compositors MAY render as a clickable reference.
A hyperlink to an external resource.
A citation reference — source + optional anchor/page.
Displays the current value of a reactive signal.
A streaming output channel (token stream, audio stream, etc.).
Present a choice between mutually exclusive options.
Request yes/no confirmation before the agent proceeds.
Request typed input from the human.
An in-flight or completed tool invocation.
The outcome of a tool call.
Bounded progress — optional percentage + optional label.

A filesystem read. The `content` slot fills in when the read completes.

Lifecycle — mirrors the `ToolCall` pattern: - `NodeAdded` with `content: None` when the agent decides to read. Paired lifecycle status `pending`. - `NodeUpdated { patch: { intent: FileRead { content: Some(...) } } }` when the content lands. Lifecycle moves to `resolved`.

For very large reads the agent MAY pair this with a sibling `Stream` intent and populate `content` only on `StreamChunk { final_: true }`.

bytes.description: Byte count. Compositors may compute from `content.len()` if the emitter doesn't supply it; supplying it authoritatively is preferred for multi-byte encodings.

content.description: File content once the read completes. Absent while in-flight.

mime.description: MIME type hint — enables syntax highlighting and preview-mode switching. Compositors SHOULD assume `text/plain` when absent.

path.description: Absolute or workspace-relative path. Compositors MAY render it as a clickable target. No scheme is implied; the emitter decides.

oneOf.description:
A filesystem write. `op` narrows the kind of write (create / write / append / delete); `content` slots in when the write completes.

Lifecycle: 1. `NodeAdded` with `content: None` and `op` set. Lifecycle `pending`. 2. `NodeUpdated` patching `content` (and `bytes` if the emitter knows them) when the write resolves. Lifecycle `resolved` on success, `failed` on error.

For streamed writes, pair with a sibling `Stream` intent and patch `content` on stream completion.

bytes.description: Byte count — authoritative when supplied, else derive from `content.len()`.

content.description: The full body written. Absent until the write resolves. SHOULD be absent for `FileWriteKind::Delete`.

op.description: The kind of write. Constrained via `FileWriteKind`.

title.description: Optional human-readable title the agent chose for this artifact. Useful when `path` is a synthetic workspace path and `title` is the user-facing name the compositor should surface.

oneOf.description: A container whose `GroupKind` tells the compositor how to lay out children.
A named section — compositors MAY render a heading; children are the body.
A visual break between siblings.
A scalar or vector field, indexed by coordinate, projected for the chosen surface.
A named spatial anchor in the given frame of reference.
A coherent arrangement of agents (quorum, swarm, formation).
A bitmap or vector image.
An audio source. Either a pre-rendered URI, a live stream, or a TTS voice.
A video source.
Intentionally empty (useful as a container whose content will stream in).
Compositor-specific or experimental intents. Compositors that don't know `kind` SHOULD fall back to rendering a generic placeholder with the payload preview.

IntentProfile.description: Overall presentation bias for a scene.

oneOf.description: Default — balanced density and ambience.
Dense technical output — maximize information per unit surface.
Ambient monitor — minimize intrusion, prefer peripheral rendering.
Cinematic — long-form narrative, one idea per frame.
Conversational — paced for human back-and-forth.

Lifecycle.description: Temporal and attentional state of a node.

created_at.description: When this node was first emitted by the agent.

expires_at.description: Optional expiration. Compositors SHOULD remove or fade past this time.

priority.description: Attention priority — shapes placement, color, and notification behavior.

status.description: Current status in the node's lifecycle.

Node.description: A node in the Prosopon IR tree.

attrs.description: Free-form attribute bag for hints the compositor MAY use. Well-known keys: `emphasis` (`"low" | "normal" | "high"`), `semantic_role` (e.g. `"error"`, `"success"`), `width_hint` (fraction 0..=1), `voice` (TTS voice id).

NodeStatus.description: Discrete lifecycle state of a node.

oneOf.description: Node is live and interactive.
Node is waiting on an upstream process (tool call, user response).
Node's intent has been satisfied — useful for collapsing completed items.
Node failed — `reason` is human-readable and SHOULD be surfaced in the UI.
Node is fading out according to a decay curve (0.0 = fresh, 1.0 = gone).
Background presence — persistent but non-demanding (e.g. a live metric).
Default rendering priority.
Requires user attention soon but does not block other flows.
Halts dependent flows until resolved (e.g. a destructive-action confirm).

Projection.description: How a field is projected into the compositor's surface.

oneOf.description: Flat 2D heatmap.
Contour lines.
3D volume rendering — only honored by 3D compositors.
Reduced to a scalar summary (min/mean/max) by the compositor.

SceneHints.description: Compositor-steering hints carried with every scene.

intent_profile.description: Overall intent profile — tells compositors whether to bias dense+technical or sparse+ambient presentations.

preferred_surfaces.description: Ordered preferred surfaces, most preferred first. A compositor MAY skip a scene if its surface is not listed; compositors SHOULD respect the first match.

viewport.description: Rough viewport budget — hints for truncation/layout. `None` = unbounded.

Severity.description: Severity level for confirmation dialogs, warnings, and alerts.

oneOf.description: Just the current value, formatted by the compositor.
A miniature plot (sparkline, bar).
A badge (colored chip with value).

SignalRef.description: A reference to a live value. Supports nested paths for structured signals: `SignalRef { topic: "plexus.state", path: ["quorum", "ratio"] }` addresses the `ratio` field inside the `quorum` object inside the `plexus.state` signal.

SignalValue.description:
A concrete signal value pushed onto the bus at a given instant.

Compositors MAY cache last-known values to render bindings when a signal has not yet emitted.

SpatialFrame.description: Frame of reference for a spatial locus.

oneOf.description: Viewer-centric — `position` is in the compositor's viewport space.
Scene-world — `position` is in the shared world coordinate system.
Earth-fixed — `position` is (lat, lon, alt).
Token-by-token text (LLM generation, log tail).
Audio PCM or encoded.
Arbitrary binary frames.
Structured JSON lines.

SurfaceKind.description: The shape of surface a compositor renders onto.

oneOf.description: Plain ANSI / text terminal or plain-text document.
2D raster or vector canvas (browser DOM, native 2D).
3D world (WebGPU, native 3D engines).
GPU shader program — agents become field/material parameters.
Audio playback surface (speech, ambient music, sonification).
Spatial computing (Vision Pro / Quest / AR).
Haptic / tactile feedback.

TimePoint.description: Single point in a time-series signal.

Transform.description:
Optional transformation applied to the signal value before binding.

Transforms are intentionally limited to pure, deterministic functions so compositors can execute them safely without a scripting runtime.

oneOf.description: Pass the value through unchanged.
Format the value into a template string. `{}` is replaced with the value.
Clamp a numeric value into `[min, max]`.
Round a numeric value to `decimals` places.
Multiply by 100 and append `"%"`.

Viewport.description: Rough viewport budget. Compositors with bounded surfaces (terminals, small screens) SHOULD respect these; surfaces without a natural viewport (audio, spatial) MAY ignore.

Visibility.description: Whether this slot should be offered to the user right now.

oneOf.description: Rendered but visually de-emphasized.
Not rendered; still in the IR for programmatic inspection.
